repository.name: dispatch
repository.description: Reliable event delivery for multi-region commerce systems.

pullRequest.title: Make webhook replay claims atomic
pullRequest.body:
## Context

During the June 28 incident, two workers could replay the same failed delivery because ownership was recorded after the status check.

## Changes

- claim replay ownership in Postgres before delivery
- recover claims after a bounded lease
- expose claim state in the operations console
- cover concurrent workers and document the recovery path

## Rollout

Ship behind `atomic_replay_claims`, enable in one region, then expand after the duplicate-delivery metric remains flat for 24 hours.
pullRequest.author: nina-sato

commits.messageHeadline: Use database time for replay leases

messages.author: user
messages.bodyMarkdown: Does a crashed worker strand this delivery forever, or can another worker reclaim it after the lease expires?

messages.author: agent
messages.bodyMarkdown:
Another worker can reclaim it after `claimed_until` passes. The `ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` remains atomic: only the worker whose claim observes an expired lease receives the returned row.

The risk is the time source. `excluded.claimed_at` currently comes from the worker, so clock drift between regions can shorten or extend the effective lease.

messages.author: user
messages.bodyMarkdown: Can two regions disagree if their worker clocks drift?

messages.author: agent
messages.bodyMarkdown: Yes. Revision 2 closes that gap by reading `transaction_timestamp()` from Postgres before claiming and by covering two workers with intentionally skewed application clocks. Lease comparison and lease creation now share one authoritative clock.

agentTurns.responseBodyMarkdown:
Another worker can reclaim it after `claimed_until` passes. The `ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` remains atomic: only the worker whose claim observes an expired lease receives the returned row.

The risk is the time source. `excluded.claimed_at` currently comes from the worker, so clock drift between regions can shorten or extend the effective lease.
agentTurns.threadSummaryUpdate: Replay claims are recoverable after lease expiry; database time should govern cross-region leases.

agentTurns.responseBodyMarkdown: Yes. Revision 2 closes that gap by reading `transaction_timestamp()` from Postgres before claiming and by covering two workers with intentionally skewed application clocks. Lease comparison and lease creation now share one authoritative clock.
agentTurns.threadSummaryUpdate: Database time now governs replay claim leases across regions.
